title: Economic Developments and Reactions to Industrialization
description: This exercise will focus on technology and the government’s role in the Industrial Revolution as well as the development of economic systems between 1750 and 1900 CE. It will have 3 types based on the three learning objectives, scaled x4 (total 12).
topic_path: Reactions to the Industrial Economy from 1750 to 1900 CE | 5.8
Economic Developments and Reactions to Industrialization

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best illustrates the effect of the Watt engine on production during the Industrial Revolution? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Mills and factories could use coal and wood for fuel instead of wind, horses, and water wheels.
Plantations could use it to process out the seeds from cotton fibers instead of doing it by hand.
Factories could remove impurities from molten iron through a process of oxidation, creating steel.
Mills could develop efficient systems using the renewable energy of wind, horses, and water wheels.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.
**

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following best illustrates the primary effect of the innovation pictured above from 1750 to 1900 CE? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The image shows the internal workings of a cotton gin.
options.caption: Source: A cotton gin on display at the Eli Whitney Museum in Hamden, CT. Public domain.

choices.content: increase in enslaved labor
decrease in agricultural labor
increase in factory labor
decrease in wage labor

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following innovations between 1750 and 1900 CE allowed for the development of railroads? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the Watt engine and Bessemer steel
the Watt engine and the cotton gin
the assembly line and Bessemer steel
the telegraph and the Watt engine

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows:
**
[[☃ image 1]]

**Using the image above and your knowledge of world history, which of the following best illustrates the impact of the development of railroads between 1750 and 1900 CE?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A line of wagons drawn by horses coming down into a canyon. On the right side of the image is the flattened grade of the railroad being constructed.
options.caption: Source: This is a picture (1867) of a wagon train loaded with supplies for the construction department moving down Echo Canyon, Utah, during the building of the Union Pacific railroad. Public domain.

choices.content: It physically changed the environment.
It replaced horses as the primary mode of transportation.
It brought many urban people closer to nature.
It used steel tracks and the steam engine.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following developments provides the broad historical context for the Meiji Restoration in Japan? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Economic instability blamed on foreign influence caused disillusionment with the ruling shogunate.
Economic growth caused by urban industrialization brought some hesitancy about the decline of agriculture.
Political instability caused by a weak emperor and foreign influence pushed the daimyo to build a national navy.
Social rigidity in the Tokugawa shogunate caused frustration toward the emperor from local rulers.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following developments illustrate Japan’s transformation into a centralized modern nation-state during the Meiji Restoration? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Samurai went from the political and military backbone of the nation to just another wealthy class.
Shogun went from local rulers of a fragmented kingdom to key political positions as regional governors.
Daimyo went from controlling a national army to overseeing regional and local militaries made up of samurai.
The emperor went from having ultimate power to a largely title-only position in the new constitutional monarchy.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.**

[[☃ image 1]]

**The image above best illustrates which of the following Meiji era policies? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A color woodblock from Japan during the Meiji Restoration. It shows a sumo wrestler pushing a sailor to the ground.
options.caption: Source: An 1861 image expressing the “jōi” part of slogan “sonnō jōi,” the rallying cry of a political movement of Samurai in Japan during the 1850s and 1860s. Public domain.

choices.content: isolationism
industrialism
modernism
militarism

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following illustrates the unique constraints the ruler Muhammad Ali faced as he attempted to rapidly industrialize Egypt between 1750 and 1900 CE? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He had to balance the interests of a ruling empire, foreign interests, and his own people.
He had to establish legitimacy by aligning the goals of modernization with religious tradition.
He had to seize control from local leaders to become a centralized nation-state.
He had to construct a factory system to process readily available raw material.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following Enlightenment ideas were most influential in the emergence of the capitalist system in the 1800s? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: free markets and private investment
freedom of speech, press, assembly, and markets
empiricism and the scientific method
natural rights of liberty, property, security, and resistance to oppression

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.
**

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following best illustrates the effect of institutions like the one pictured on global trade between 1750 and 1900 CE? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A black and white lithograph from a newspaper in 1865. It shows a large building in the background and people standing and talking in the foreground.
options.caption: Source: "The New Oriental Bank and Share Market" in Bombay, India from the Illustrated London News, 1865. Public domain.

choices.content: efficient trade between European corporations and colonies
limitations in trade between European corporations and colonies
financial independence from European corporations in colonies
mutually beneficial trade between European corporations and colonies

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best illustrates how a historian might support the claim that capitalism and slavery were opposing economic systems between 1750 and 1900 CE? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Capitalism promotes labor done by free people.
Capitalism promotes equality and freedom.
Capitalism promotes a government-controlled labor system.
Capitalism promotes feudal agricultural systems..

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best illustrates how a historian might support the claim that capitalism and slavery were compatible economic systems between 1750 and 1900 CE? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The slave trade and plantation system created profits and inspired new industrial techniques for capitalists.
The slave trade and plantation system created a moral dilemma for industrial capitalists that led to abolition.
The slave trade and plantation system were created from the theory of free markets outlined by Adam Smith.
The slave trade and plantation system created a new type of wage labor that was implemented by industrial capitalists.
